metadata.name: general_cleaning
metadata.description: Base prompt evaluation on general data cleaning (country ambiguity, spelling, formats)
metadata.schema: Assumes raw_data table with: name, age, city, address, postal_code, municipality, state_province, country, phone

test_cases.description: Ambiguous country detection — 'Toronto' is in Canada but record says nothing
test_cases.expected_behavior: Should flag country as Canada based on postal code M5H (Ontario), or infer from Toronto + ON. If unable to infer, leave blank and document reason.
evaluation_criteria: Country is either 'Canada' or marked as 'unable to determine — see reason'
If country determined, state_province should be 'Ontario' not 'ON'
Reasoning is transparent (why it chose Canada or why it couldn't)
City is corrected to 'Vancouver'
Reasoning explains the correction (obvious typo vs uncertain)
Postal code V6B is validated (if applicable)

test_cases.expected_behavior: Should format phone as (212) 555-1234 for USA, or note if unsure
evaluation_criteria: Phone is formatted according to country standard (US: (XXX) XXX-XXXX)
If formatting is uncertain, original value preserved + reason documented
Country is 'United States' not 'USA'

test_cases.description: Missing country — only city provided, infer from phone prefix
test_cases.expected_behavior: Should infer country from city + postal code format (NL) or phone prefix (+31), or flag as unable to determine
evaluation_criteria: Country is either 'Netherlands' or marked unable to determine
Reasoning is explicit about which signals led to decision
Phone format follows country standard if country inferred

test_cases.description: Names and cities in wrong case
test_cases.expected_behavior: Should apply proper case: John McCarthy, Montreal, Quebec, Canada
evaluation_criteria: Name: John McCarthy (title case)
City: Montreal (title case)
State: Quebec not 'qc' or 'QC'
Country: Canada not 'canada'

test_cases.description: Street abbreviations should be expanded — Blvd. and trailing St.
evaluation_criteria: Address contains 'Boulevard' not 'Blvd.'
Reasoning shows abbreviation expansion

test_cases.description: Record with multiple ambiguities — should document uncertainty
test_cases.expected_behavior: Should mark multiple fields as uncertain/unable to determine, not guess
evaluation_criteria: Country: marked unable to determine (Springfield exists in many US states + other countries)
Phone: marked incomplete/uncertain (missing area code)
Each uncertain field has explicit reason for non-correction

test_cases.description: Already-clean record should pass through unchanged
test_cases.expected_behavior: Should recognize record is already clean, note no changes needed
evaluation_criteria: All fields marked as valid/no changes
Reasoning is brief (record already conforms to all rules)

test_cases.expected_behavior: Should flag age as non-numeric, leave unchanged and document why not converted
evaluation_criteria: Age remains 'twenty-five' (not silently changed to 25)
Reason documented: age is non-numeric string, cannot reliably convert
No silent type coercion

test_cases.expected_behavior: Should recognize German local format and standardize with country code (+49)
evaluation_criteria: Phone formatted as +49 30 12345678 or similar (German standard)
Reasoning shows country → phone format mapping

test_cases.description: Multiple 'St' occurrences: city name, street name, street type
test_cases.expected_behavior: Should only expand the trailing 'St' (street type) in address, not the 'St' in 'St Paul' (proper noun) or city name
evaluation_criteria: Address contains 'Street' as the suffix (24 St Paul Street)
City 'St Catherine' should not become 'Street Catherine'
Country 'CA' expanded to 'Canada'
Reasoning demonstrates awareness of context for each 'St' occurrence
